_meta.description: Project type profiles with dimension weight overrides, skipped/critical items, and template recommendations.
_meta.usage: Load the matching profile to adjust audit weights and skip irrelevant items. All 8 weights are explicit per profile and sum to 1.0.

frontend-spa.name: Frontend SPA

substitute_items.3.2: Client-side error tracking and performance monitoring (Sentry, LogRocket, etc.)

frontend-spa.notes: UI visibility (3.4) and E2E testing (4.5) are high priority. Dependency direction applies to component architecture.

frontend-ssr.name: Frontend SSR/SSG
frontend-ssr.description: Server-side rendered or static site (Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro)
frontend-ssr.notes: Hybrid rendering means both server observability and client E2E matter.

backend-api.name: Backend API Service
backend-api.description: REST/GraphQL API service or web server

substitute_items.3.4: API response inspection and endpoint health monitoring

backend-api.notes: Observability and safety are paramount. All Dim 3 items except UI visibility apply fully.

backend-microservice.name: Backend Microservice

substitute_items.3.4: Service mesh visibility and inter-service tracing

backend-microservice.notes: Distributed tracing (3.2) and agent-queryable observability (3.3) are essential.

fullstack.name: Full-Stack Application
fullstack.description: Monolithic full-stack app (frontend + backend in one repo)
fullstack.notes: Default weights apply. Dependency direction between frontend/backend layers is critical.

library.name: Library / Package
library.description: Reusable library published to npm, PyPI, crates.io, Maven, etc.

substitute_items.3.4: Can the agent see tool output and error messages clearly?

library.notes: Testing and mechanical constraints are the most important. Observability items for services do not apply.

cli-tool.name: CLI Tool

substitute_items.3.4: Can the agent see CLI output, exit codes, and stderr clearly?

cli-tool.notes: Focus on clippy/lint, type safety, test coverage, and diagnostic error context.

desktop-app.name: Desktop Application
desktop-app.description: Desktop app (Electron, Tauri, Qt, WPF, SwiftUI, native)
desktop-app.notes: UI visibility and E2E testing are critical. Architecture boundary enforcement matters for multi-process apps.

mobile-app.name: Mobile Application
mobile-app.description: Mobile app (React Native, Flutter, SwiftUI, Kotlin/Compose, native iOS/Android)

substitute_items.3.2: Mobile crash analytics and performance monitoring (Firebase, Sentry)
substitute_items.3.3: Device log access and remote debugging capability

mobile-app.notes: Device testing, UI automation, and architecture boundaries are key.

system-infra.name: System / Infrastructure
system-infra.description: OS-level code, drivers, embedded systems, kernel modules
system-infra.notes: Safety rails and rollback are critical. Type safety and mechanical constraints are paramount.

game.name: Game
game.description: Game project (Unity, Unreal, Godot, custom engine)

substitute_items.3.2: In-game debug console and profiler integration

game.notes: Architecture docs are crucial (engine vs gameplay separation). Cache-friendly design matters due to large asset pipelines. Safety for asset integrity.

data-ml.name: Data / ML Pipeline
data-ml.description: Data processing, ML training, ETL pipelines, Jupyter notebooks
data-ml.notes: Long-running task support is critical (training jobs). Durable execution and progress tracking are essential.

devops-iac.name: DevOps / IaC
devops-iac.description: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Pulumi, Ansible, CloudFormation, Helm)

substitute_items.3.4: Terraform plan output review and drift detection

devops-iac.notes: Safety rails are the most critical dimension. Rollback, human confirmation, and least-privilege are paramount.

script-automation.name: Script / Automation
script-automation.description: Utility scripts, cron jobs, one-off automation, build tools
script-automation.notes: Keep it simple. Lint, test, and safety basics. Long-running support matters for scheduled jobs.

browser-extension.name: Browser Extension

substitute_items.3.2: Extension background page logging and devtools integration

browser-extension.notes: Security is elevated due to browser API access. E2E testing with browser automation is essential.

smart-contract.name: Smart Contract / Blockchain
smart-contract.description: Solidity, Rust (Solana), Move, CosmWasm smart contracts

substitute_items.3.2: On-chain event monitoring and transaction tracing
substitute_items.3.4: Contract interaction testing via local blockchain (Hardhat, Foundry)

smart-contract.notes: Immutable deployments make safety and testing the most critical dimensions. Coverage thresholds should be very high.

monorepo.name: Monorepo
monorepo.description: Multi-package repository (pnpm workspaces, Turborepo, Nx, Lerna, Cargo workspace, Go modules)
monorepo.notes: Cross-package boundary enforcement is critical. Entropy management matters more due to scale. See references/monorepo-patterns.md for audit flow.
